name: Taliesin Extended
description: Taliesin, whose name means 'shining brow,' is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology and tradition, renowned as a poet and magician. His origins are shrouded in mystery and magic, typically associated with the story of Gwion Bach, who consumed the cauldron of Cerridwen's brew of wisdom and transformation. After accidentally ingesting the potion intended for Cerridwen's son, Gwion Bach gained immense knowledge and prophetic abilities. To escape Cerridwen's wrath, he transformed into various animals, ultimately becoming a grain of wheat that she consumed. Reborn as Taliesin, he possessed unparalleled poetic skill and foresight. Taliesin served as a court poet for several kings, including King Arthur and King Urien of Rheged. He is credited with composing numerous poems attributed to him, reflecting themes of history, myth, nature, and prophecy. His work is a cornerstone of Welsh literature, embodying the spirit of the Celtic bards and their connection to the Otherworld. Taliesin's enduring legacy lies in his embodiment of wisdom, transformation, and the power of poetic expression. He is a symbol of the bardic tradition and the deep connection between the Welsh people and their land, history, and mythology. He also had a strong connection with the Otherworld, and possessed gifts of prophecy and shapeshifting. He is often depicted as a wise advisor and a powerful figure, capable of both inspiring and challenging those around him. Taliesin's poetry often contains riddles and hidden meanings, reflecting his deep understanding of the world and his ability to see beyond the surface of things. He is a complex and multifaceted figure, whose story continues to resonate with readers and scholars today.
subtitle: The Shining Brow, Master Poet

keyMyths.title: The Cauldron of Cerridwen
keyMyths.description: Gwion Bach accidentally consumes the brew of wisdom prepared by Cerridwen, granting him immense knowledge and transforming him into Taliesin. This event marks his birth as the legendary poet. The source is the Hanes Taliesin.

keyMyths.title: Service to Kings
keyMyths.description: Taliesin served as a court poet to various kings, including King Arthur and King Urien of Rheged. He provided counsel and composed poems in their honor, solidifying his status as a wise advisor. The source is various Welsh texts.

keyMyths.title: Prophecies and Riddles
keyMyths.description: Taliesin's poetry is filled with prophecies and riddles, reflecting his deep understanding of the world and his ability to see beyond the surface. His words often contain hidden meanings. The source is The Book of Taliesin.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
sources.description: A collection of Welsh tales, including the story of Taliesin.
sources.text: A collection of Welsh tales, including the story of Taliesin.

sources.author: John Matthews
sources.work: Taliesin: Shamanism and the Bardic Mysteries in Britain and Ireland
sources.description: An exploration of Taliesin's role as a shamanic figure.
sources.text: An exploration of Taliesin's role as a shamanic figure.
